reasoning_chain_text: 1. For the city page in dataset position 1, identify who founded its first permanent settlement.
2. For the county page in dataset position 2, determine whether the county seat has the same name as the county.
3. For the county page in dataset position 3, record the year the county was created.
4. For the county page in dataset position 4, determine whether the county seat has a different name from the county.
5. For the county page in dataset position 5, determine whether the county seat has a different name from the county.
6. Using FY 2023 disability compensation data, flag the counties with fewer than 15,000 recipients for use with the later county filters.
7. For fiscal year 2018 Texas expenditures, identify counties with highway construction spending between $150 million and $500 million.
8. For fiscal year 2019 Texas expenditures, identify counties with highway construction spending in that same range.
9. For fiscal year 2020 Texas expenditures, identify counties with highway construction spending in that same range.

10. For fiscal year 2021 Texas expenditures, intersect the four annual county sets, apply the county-seat and disability filters from earlier steps, and use the surviving county-seat city and linked county page to retrieve the requested year.
original_final_question: Consider the only engineering firm founded after 1914 that has no current NY State construction manager projects, yet consistently ranked in the top 20 Washington State contractors by contract count annually from 2017 to 2020. In what year was the Native American interpreter born who helped negotiate the land cession that led to the founding of the city where that firm is based? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: Texas counties with highway construction $150M-$500M in FY 2018 -> {MONTGOMERY, TRAVIS, BEXAR, TARRANT, DENTON, NUECES, HAYS} (7 counties)
Node 2: Texas counties with highway construction $150M-$500M in FY 2019 -> {TRAVIS, MONTGOMERY, BEXAR, DENTON, NUECES, TARRANT, WILLIAMSON} (7 counties)
Node 3: Texas counties with highway construction $150M-$500M in FY 2020 -> {BEXAR, DENTON, NUECES, HAYS, TARRANT, MCLENNAN, WILLIAMSON} (7 counties)
Node 4: Texas counties with highway construction $150M-$500M in FY 2021 -> {BEXAR, DENTON, HAYS, TARRANT, NUECES} (5 counties)
Intersection of nodes 1-4: Counties meeting criterion all 4 years -> {BEXAR, DENTON, NUECES, TARRANT}
HOP 2 (d=4, mixed data.gov + Wikipedia - nodes 5-8 filter the intersection):
Node 5 (data.gov): VA disability recipients for intersection counties -> BEXAR (86,151), DENTON (14,716), NUECES (10,231), TARRANT (37,476)
Node 6 (Wikipedia): Denton County seat -> Denton (SAME name as county)
Node 7 (Wikipedia): Nueces County seat -> Corpus Christi (DIFFERENT name)
Node 8 (Wikipedia): Tarrant County seat -> Fort Worth (DIFFERENT name)
Filter: County seat differs from county name -> {NUECES, TARRANT}
Node 9: Nueces County seat is Corpus Christi -> Founded by Henry Lawrence Kinney in 1839
Node 10: Texas county named after Henry Lawrence Kinney -> Kinney County, created in 1850
